behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all key paths (src/zeroband/training/ckpt.py, weights.py, orchestrator/ckpt.py, README.md), class names (CheckpointManager), fields (resume_step, save_async, rollout_path, weights_path, clean), Progress dataclass fields, literal strings (pytorch_model.bin, step_{n}), and lists the specific tests that must pass.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to build (classes, fields, methods) but leaves implementation details to the agent.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for presence of class names, required fields, literal strings, and behavior via existing test compatibility — alternative correct implementations would still pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute pytest subprocesses, run ruff, import modules, and compile Python files, not just text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actual unit test suites (test_config.py etc.) to pass, which would fail against stubs missing the required config fields and classes.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover syntax, imports, TOML validity, ruff lint, and the specified unit test files listed in instruction.md section 6.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates pass_to_pass / static) and have clear docstrings per function.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code/config changes, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and names in instruction match those referenced in tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names files to modify but this is appropriate scaffolding; no specific line numbers or exact code replacements given.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code task, not an agentmd-edits task.
